description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who is automating the provisioning of host-level firewalls.  
Create the following two artefacts with **one single compound shell command** (chaining with “&&” or “;” is fine, but everything must be typed on just one command line that you execute once).

1. A rule file at  
   /home/user/firewall/allow-8080.rule  
   that contains **exactly one line**:  
   -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT  

2. A setup log at  
   /home/user/firewall/setup.log  
   that contains **exactly two lines** in this order:  
   • Line 1: today’s date produced by the command `date +%F` (e.g. 2025-02-04)  
   • Line 2: the literal word `RuleCreated` (case-sensitive)

Directory /home/user/firewall does not yet exist, so your single command must also create it.

After running the command, you can verify success with:  
cat /home/user/firewall/allow-8080.rule  
cat /home/user/firewall/setup.log
truth:
Expected file system state after successful completion:

1. Path: /home/user/firewall/allow-8080.rule  
   Permissions: default umask (0644 is acceptable).  
   Content (single line, no trailing spaces, ends with a single newline):  
   -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT

2. Path: /home/user/firewall/setup.log  
   Permissions: default umask.  
   Content (two lines, each terminated by a single newline):  
   YYYY-MM-DD  
   RuleCreated  

   where YYYY-MM-DD is the calendar date when the agent executes the task (produced by `date +%F` in the container’s locale & timezone).

File existence and exact contents—including the newline counts—will be checked by the grader.
name: task_000000_762089ed
